exemplar.title:
How does the air pressure inside a size 7 basketball (4, 6, 8, 10, 12 psi) affect its coefficient
of restitution measured through its rebound height

detailedExemplarData.title:
How does the air pressure inside a size 7 basketball (4, 6, 8, 10, 12 psi) affect its coefficient

subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Data tables and graphs are both clear and precise, with units, significant figures, uncertainties, error bars, best-fit equations, and RÂ² values.
Processing is carried out accurately, with sample calculations matching tabulated results.
Consideration of uncertainties is appropriate and propagated through all key results.
weaknesses: Justification for imposing a Â±0.03 m uncertainty floor lacks citation to instrument specification.
Outlier detection and rationale for frame-selection criteria are not described.
Minor rounding inconsistencies in uncertainty propagation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The communication of recording and processing is exemplaryâtables and graphs are both clear and precise. Uncertainties are appropriately considered and propagated, and data processing is accurate aside from minor rounding and documentation omissions. These qualities fulfill all descriptors for the 5-6 band, meriting the maximum 6/6.

strengths: Context is highly specific (basketball performance) and effectively motivates the research question.
Methodology is described step-by-step with enough precision for full reproducibility.
Theoretical foundation (energy conversion, CoR derivation) is integrated and linked to measurements.
weaknesses: Controlled-variable discussions lack quantitative justification of impact magnitudes.
Risk assessment section is incomplete, cutting off midâsentence.
Minor methodological assumptions (e.g., air resistance) are not justified.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands clearly meet the 5-6 band: the research question is situated in a precise sport context and the procedural description is unambiguous and reproducible. The third strand (âmethodological considerationsâ) is described well but lacks some deeper quantitative explanation. However, the overall coherence, depth of theoretical linkage, and clarity justify a top-band award of 6/6, reflecting an outstanding design with only minor omissions.

strengths: Conclusion is clearly stated, numerically supported, and fully consistent with the data analysis.
Hypothesis support is explicitly referenced with RÂ² values and figure citations.
External literature is cited to contextualize the observed trend.
weaknesses: Comparison to external study is concise and lacks quantitative alignment of gradients.
Discussion of the significance of RÂ² values and gradient differences is brief.
Broader implications for the sport context are not fully elaborated.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion is justified by the data and coherent with the hypothesis, meeting descriptors for a 5-6 band. However, the scientific context comparison, while relevant, is not developed in quantitative depth required for a perfect 6. Thus a solid 5/6 accurately reflects strong performance with room to deepen justification.

strengths: Specific methodological weaknesses (camera angle, frame rate limitations) are identified and linked to potential measurement bias.
Suggested improvements are realistic and relevant (origin marker, spirit level, higher-fps camera).
weaknesses: Improvements are described qualitatively without quantifying expected error reduction.
The relative impact of each weakness is discussed briefly but not in detail.
No prioritization of which limitation most affects the results.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The evaluation meets all descriptors for the 3-4 band: specific weaknesses are described and realistic improvements are suggested. However, the lack of quantitative justification for the proposed improvements and limited discussion of their relative impact prevents award of a 5-6 band. A score of 4/6 accurately captures this level of evaluation.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student opens with a clear rationale for studying basketball bounce, links this to practical play considerations, and immediately narrows the lens to the coefficient of restitution of a size-7 ball over a defined pressure range.  Background theory (energy conversion, definition of CoR) is integrated and explicitly connected to the planned measurement method (drop-rebound).  Because the context is sport-specific, pressure range is justified, and the link between theory and investigation is explicit, the description meets the descriptor of âspecific and appropriate contextâ and fully exceeds the outline required for the 3-4 band.

Uncertainties are recorded for every raw measurement, propagated through mean heights and CoR, and displayed as error bars and gradient uncertainties.  The propagation method is shown in a sample calculation.  One weakness is the arbitrary floor of Â±0.03 m imposed on small uncertainties, which is not fully justified.  Thus consideration is largely appropriate but not flawless, meriting a solid 5 within the top band.

The student identifies all key variables, provides justifications for controls, and briefly explains why each might affect rebound height.  However, discussion stops at the descriptive level; there is limited elaboration of how large each effect could be or why chosen control ranges are sufficient.  The considerations are therefore âdescribedâ rather than fully âexplainedâ, aligning with the 3-4 band.

Two concrete methodological weaknesses (camera angle/systematic origin error; limited frame rate) are identified and the underlying causes are described.  The narrative notes how each could skew rebound height measurements, indicating understanding of relative impact, but the discussion remains brief and does not quantify the magnitude of error.  This aligns with the 3-4 descriptor for âdescribes specific weaknessesâ.

Step-by-step instructions (pump setting, ruler placement, number of bounces recorded, video-analysis procedure) are articulated in sufficient detail that another student could replicate the work without clarification.  Instrument placement distances, drop height reference, number of frames analysed, and trial repetition are all stated.  No significant ambiguities in quantities or equipment remain, so the description is fully reproducible, satisfying the top-band requirement.

Averages, standard deviations, percentage uncertainties, CoR values and logarithmic/linear regression are carried out correctly; sample calculations are shown and match tabulated results.  Minor rounding inconsistencies appear (e.g., CoR uncertainty rounding), preventing a perfect execution, yet the processing is otherwise accurate and appropriate, fitting the 5 mark level in the top band.

Improvements (marker for origin, spirit level, higher-fps camera) directly tackle the stated limitations and are feasible.  The explanation of how each improvement would enhance accuracy is present but limited to qualitative statements; no detailed justification or predicted error reduction is offered, so the strand fits the âdescribedâ rather than âexplainedâ level, warranting 4 marks.

Raw and processed data tables carry units, significant figures, and explicit uncertainties; graphs include correctly-scaled axes, error bars, best-fit equations and RÂ² values.  Narrative text clearly links data to visuals.  Both clarity (easy to follow) and precision (consistent sig. figs., labelled uncertainties) are demonstrated across the full dataset, fulfilling the highest descriptor.

A single external study (Kareem & Kim 2009) is cited to corroborate the logarithmic trend, and the impossibility of CoR > 1 is invoked.  Although relevant, the comparison is concise and not deeply analysed (e.g., no quantitative alignment of gradients).  This meets âsome relevant comparisonâ but falls short of the sustained justification expected for the 5-6 band.

The student explicitly states that the hypothesis is supported, quotes numerical CoR changes, references figures and RÂ² values, and explains why a logarithmic model is selected.  The conclusion is fully consistent with the analysed data but the discussion of significance and quantitative comparison (e.g., gradient meaning) is brief, so a 5 (rather than 6) best reflects the quality.

annotations.comment: The research question is clear but could better highlight the practical performance implications for players, such as dribble control or shot consistency, to deepen contextual justification.
Equations for GPE and KE are correctly presented and referenced, providing a solid theoretical foundation for relating drop height to rebound velocity.
The text assumes negligible air resistance without justification. A brief discussion of its magnitude relative to gravitational forces would strengthen methodological rigor.
Figure 1 clearly illustrates the energy loss during collision, reinforcing the narrative on deformation and dissipation mechanisms.

The introduction skillfully integrates the basketball context with the investigationâs aim, explicitly linking energy conversion and CoR measurement through rebound heights. This specific context grounds the research question effectively.
Listing all materials and apparatus promotes reproducibility by specifying equipment needed for each stage.
The list omits specification of the camera model or resolution, which could affect frame rate and measurement precision. Adding this detail would improve reproducibility.
The risk assessment correctly identifies overinflation hazards, but the description cuts off midâsentence. Completing this section ensures safety considerations are fully addressed.
The CoR definition is stated clearly, but quantitative discussion of energy dissipation channels (heat, sound) is omitted. Including approximate loss fractions would deepen understanding.
The derivation of CoR in terms of rebound and initial heights is methodical and clearly links theory to measurable quantities.
The hypothesis attributes increased rebound to molecular collisions but omits reference to the ideal gas law (PV=nRT). Incorporating that would strengthen the physical rationale.
The independent variable range (4â12 psi) is well justified, ensuring sufficient variation to observe a clear trend in CoR values.
The controlled variables table is detailed, but the method to minimize atmospheric pressure fluctuations could specify target pressure stability or logged pressure ranges.
The experimental procedure is described step-by-step, allowing replication without ambiguity in equipment placement or sequence.
The data analysis steps note origin setting, but lack detail on frame selection criteria (e.g. number of frames per bounce) for consistent height measurement.
The raw data table is well organized with clear headings and consistent units, facilitating further analysis.
There is no mention of outlier detection or treatment in the raw data. Implementing a clear criterion (e.g. Â±2Ï) would strengthen data processing transparency.
Sample calculation for average rebound height is clearly shown with correct formula notation and rounding to appropriate significant figures.
The CoR uncertainty propagation uses percentage uncertainties but shows minor rounding inconsistencies. Ensuring uniform rounding rules for each step would improve precision.
Replacing sub-0.03 m uncertainties with Â±0.03 m is appropriate, but the justification relies on an unreferenced estimate. Cite Logger Proâs specification or calibration data.
Figures 2 and 3 include clear axes labels and error bars, effectively communicating the processed data relationships.
The narrative describing RÂ² values is incompleteâonly one RÂ² is stated explicitly. Including both values and their implications would strengthen interpretation.
The conclusion succinctly restates findings with numerical support and references to figures, demonstrating consistency between data and interpretation.
The scientific context comparison cites one external study but lacks quantitative alignment of gradients. A deeper discussion of how your RÂ² values compare numerically would enhance justification.
The evaluation identifies specific systematic errors with clear descriptions of their impact on origin setting and height measurement precision.
Suggested improvements are realistic but limited to qualitative statements. Quantifying the expected reduction in error from higher frame rates or levelled setup would deepen evaluation.

rubricData.name: Physics IA
rubricData.rubricSpecificInfo: âThe Physics Internal Assessment (IA) is a critical component of the IB Diploma Programme, providing students with the opportunity to design and conduct independent investigations into physics phenomena. This assessment emphasizes the development of practical skills, analytical thinking, and a deep understanding of scientific methodologies. By engaging in the IA, students not only apply theoretical knowledge to real-world scenarios but also cultivate competencies in research design, data analysis, and critical evaluation, all of which are essential for academic and professional success in scientific fields. â
